notes: Phase 4d — real evals against the wired USAspending MCP. Run from a workspace whose KG has at least program/agency/NAICS context (e.g. afcap5_isr or afcap6_drfp). Live MCP eval requires THESEUS_LIVE_MCP=1.

evals.prompt: Who is the incumbent on this contract and when does their period of performance end?
Give me a black-hat read on the top three likely competitors for this opportunity. I need predicted themes and ghost language hooks I can hand to my proposal lead.
What's the typical award value range for this NAICS at this magnitude over the last 5 years? Be honest if the sample is too small to give a real range.
Run competitive intel on this opportunity.
evals.workspace_hint: use a workspace with NO regulatory_reference entities for NAICS/PSC

expectations.rule: no competitor name should appear that wasn't returned by mcp__usaspending__search_awards

evals.name: Grounding-audit floor
evals.prompt: Who is the incumbent on this contract and when does their period of performance end?
expected_signals: tools/audit_skill_grounding.py --skill competitive-intel reports grounding_ratio >= 0.6 (black-hat envelope is mostly KG-grounded incumbent claims, but ~25% of cover-note prose is competitive-strategy reasoning; the on-disk JSON envelope source_chunks are the citation-of-record, the chat narrative is the human-readable digest).
tools/audit_skill_grounding.py reports claims_exempt is at least 0.40 of (claims_total + claims_exempt).
No regression below 0.6 across at least three consecutive runs of the same prompt.
